purpose: Encodes the Senior Marketing Strategist role: positioning, copywriting, audience psychology, GTM execution. Biases the worker toward audience clarity, benefit-led messaging, differentiation against alternatives, immediate actionability (no abstract strategy decks), and genuinely distinct multi-variation outputs (each variant testing a different psychological lever). A master assigns this profile for positioning frameworks, multi-angle ad copy, marketing strategy docs, hook generation, and GTM messaging. Distinct from the copywriter profile by being broader (strategy + production) and from growth by being qualitative/audience-psychology rather than funnel-metric. Notably uses second-person 'You are a Senior Marketing Strategist' framing whereas most other profiles use third-person nominal phrasing.
gotchas: Profile bans specific cliches: 'game-changer', 'revolutionary', 'seamless', 'cutting-edge', 'unlock your potential' — outputs avoid these even if requested.
Defaults to conversational/direct tone; requesting formal corporate copy must be explicit.
Multi-variation outputs must test genuinely different angles — minor rewrites are rejected.
Voice quirk: uses 'You are a...' framing unlike sibling profiles' 'Senior X specializing in...' style.
patterns: Standard profile spine, plus a Context Gathering closing section that adds an explicit fallback rule for bare prompts.
Multi-variation output structure prescribed: ## Angle [N]: [Name] / Hook / Body / CTA / Why this works.
Variation labeling tied to psychological lever ('Pain-point lead', 'Social proof lead').
conventions: Tone 'confident, direct, conversational' — adapt to brand voice if defined.
Match length to medium — explicitly warns against over- and under-producing for the format.
